text_for_index: India's electric bus stock grew from under 3,000 in 2020 to over 11,500 by end of 2024. In 2024 India was the world's second-largest electric bus market by sales volume, with 3,200+ units sold.

metadata.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (Trends in heavy-duty EVs)

text_for_index: India's National Electric Bus Programme targets 40,000 electric buses by 2027. The Bharat Urban Megabus Mission aims to introduce 100,000 electric buses to cities with population over 1 million. The PM E-DRIVE scheme supports replacement of ageing public buses.

metadata.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (Trends in heavy-duty EVs)

text_for_index: India aims to reach a stock of 50,000 electric buses by 2027, supported by a USD 390 million fund backed by the Indian and US governments to provide loans expanding electric bus manufacturing.
IEA projects India's electric bus sales share rises to about 35% in 2030 and 60% in 2035 in both the STEPS and APS scenarios (projection, not actuals).

Global electric bus sales exceeded 70,000 units in 2024, up 30% year-over-year. Electric buses were about 3% of total global bus sales in 2023. Under stated policies the electric bus stock rises sevenfold by 2035.

metadata.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (Trends in heavy-duty EVs)

text_for_index: China deployed over 680,000 electric buses, with almost 70% installed before 2020. China's share of global electric bus sales fell from ~99% in 2017 to under 70% in 2024. China exported over 15,000 electric buses in 2024, a 25% increase versus 2023.

metadata.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (Trends in heavy-duty EVs)

text_for_index: In Europe the 2024 electric bus sales share exceeded 13% and reached nearly 50% of new city buses. In the United States electric bus sales fell ~40% from their prior peak; school buses are ~50% of stock.

metadata.source: IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (Trends in heavy-duty EVs)

text_for_index: Lithium-ion battery pack prices fell to a record low of USD 139/kWh in 2023 (-14%), USD 115/kWh in 2024 (-20%, the largest drop since 2017), and USD 108/kWh in 2025 — roughly 93% below 2010 levels (~USD 1,183/kWh). Drivers: LFP chemistry adoption, manufacturing overcapacity, economies of scale.

India had about 25,202 public EV charging stations by December 2024. The PM E-DRIVE scheme allocates Rs 10,900 crore over two years, including Rs 2,000 crore specifically for public charging stations.

metadata.source: India Ministry of Heavy Industries / PM E-DRIVE

text_for_index: Battery supply chains remain concentrated: China dominates cell manufacturing and LFP cathode supply, and holds the vast majority of the global electric bus fleet. Falling pack prices help tender economics but do not remove single-country component dependence risk for Indian OEMs.

metadata.source: BloombergNEF Battery Price Survey / IEA Global EV Outlook 2025 (Trends in heavy-duty EVs)
